mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals imminent labor market slack and recessionary pressures, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or rolling IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates stagflationary pressures that compress real discount rates and corporate margins, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in > 50% of observations when both thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate hiring, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03) across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand and impending earnings revisions, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of quarters where GDP growth crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate confirms a synchronized supply-labor recovery, boosting market confidence and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
